mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, with a negative mean return difference exceeding -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 5-day forward returns in greater than 60% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth crosses below 1.5%, aggregate demand weakness triggers recession risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 58% of quarters where GDPC1 growth remains below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms synchronized economic expansion, boosting corporate profit expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and INDPRO uptrend signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during inventory correction phases signals supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during inventory correction phases fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a mean forward return below 0.0% across all correction periods.
